channel.title: AP News 全文 RSS（简体中文版）
channel.description: AP News 首页抓取 + 英译简

item.title: 菲律宾发生里氏7.8级地震，造成至少35人死亡、建筑物倒塌并引发海啸
item.description:
DAVAO, Philippines (AP) — An offshore earthquake with a magnitude of 7.8 hit the southernPhilippineson Monday, killing at least 35 people, injuring more than 200 others mostly in ruined buildings and sending a 1-meter (3-foot)tsunamiinto nearby coasts.
Several mostly low-rise buildings collapsed or sustained heavy damages in the hard-hit city of General Santos. Tsunami damage was reported in at least one southern coastal village. Smaller waves were measured inIndonesiaand Palau and as far away as southern Japan.
In a video shared by Mahayahay Elementary School on social media, school children were seen screaming and crying when an offshore magnitude 7.8 earthquake rocked the southern Philippines early Monday.
The quake also triggered a landslide in Glan, a municipality in the province of Sarangani, that killed 13 villagers, Rene Punzalan, a provincial disaster-mitigation official, told the DZBB radio network. Four other villagers died in Sarangani, he said.
The major earthquake was thestrongest to hit the Philippinesthis year, Teresito Bacolcol, the director of the Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology, said. He warned people to seek advice before returning to damaged buildings and houses, which could collapse due to aftershocks.
The United States, a treaty ally of the Philippines, said it was coordinating with Manila and was ready to support Philippine response efforts. France, Japan and New Zealand also expressed support.
“Our pickup truck suddenly jerked and I thought we had a flat tire,” Rod Sosmeña, a regional director of the Office of Civil Defense, told The Associated Press from General Santos, where he was traveling when the quake struck at 7:37 a.m.
“The shaking was very strong and people dashed out of houses into the streets,” Sosmeña said.
More than 100 students in uniforms and a dozen teachers had gathered for a flag-raising ceremony in a coconut tree-ringed grade school compound in the rural town of Malita in Davao Occidental province when the ground shook, turning the first day of school after a two-month summer break into chaos.
“Their excitement on the first day of school turned to trauma,” school principal Rosavel Cachuela told the AP.
Some of the young students screamed in panic and wept but most remained seated and still, preventing any injuries, Cachuela said, adding that a motorcycle was damaged when a shed crumbled to the ground.
At least four people remained missing in General Santos, a port city of more than 700,000 people and a regional hub for the tuna export industry. Search and rescue teams worked to find people who may have been trapped in a supermarket, a warehouse, a grade school, and other small buildings that either collapsed or were severely damaged, officials said.
The international airport in General Santos was temporarily shut due to the earthquake and 17 domestic flights were canceled, the Civil Aviation Authority of the Philippines said.
The quake was centered at sea off Mindanao, the second most populous island in the Philippine archipelago. According to Bacolcol, the quake occurred at a depth of 33 kilometers (20 miles), about 32 kilometers (20 miles) southwest of Maasim town in Sarangani province.
President Ferdinand Marcos Jr.ordered the cancellation of classes and directed disaster-response agencies to immediately get to work in quake-hit provinces, saying “the national government is moving and we will not leave Mindanao behind.”
A magnitude 7.8 earthquake shook part of the southern Philippines early Monday, causing damage in a key coastal city, knocking down power and sparking a tsunami warning on some regional coasts.
The Pacific Tsunami Warning Center said the threat of a tsunami largely passed about five hours after the quake. Philippine officials also lifted a tsunami warning by mid-afternoon. Six shanties on stilts were damaged in a coastal village in Zamboanga del Sur province due to the quake and taller waves, officials said.
Aside from the landslide in Sarangani, most of the other deaths were caused by collapsing buildings and falling debris, including in a damaged mosque, in the southern provinces of South Cotabato and Davao Occidental, and on Balut Island, according to Sosmeña and another disaster-mitigation official, Ednar Dayanghirang.
The DZRH radio network in Manila reported that a four-story commercial building where its provincial station was located partly collapsed and staffers dashed to the ground floor without injuries.
Waves of 1 meter (3 feet) were monitored in the provinces of Sultan Kudarat and Sarangani. A 1.4-meter (4.6-foot) wave hit at one time in Kiamba town, Bacolcol said.
The quake was also felt in Malaysia’s Sabah state on Borneo island. Sabah is just a boat ride away from southern Philippines. An 83-centimeter (2.7-feet) tsunami was measured by a gauge off Indonesia’s Sulawesi island, and the PTWC said 30-centimeter (1-foot) waves were measured in Palau.
Waves up to 20 centimeters (7.8 inches) were detected on the remote Japanese island of Chichijima and the central Japanese town of Kushimoto, the Japan Meteorological Agency said.
The Philippines is often hit by earthquakes and volcanic eruptions due to its location on the Pacific “Ring of Fire,” an arc of seismic faults around the ocean.
The archipelago is also battered by about 20 typhoons and tropical storms each year, making it one of the world’s most disaster-prone countries.
Gomez reported from Manila, Philippines. Associated Press writers Edna Tarigan in Jakarta, Indonesia, Eileen Ng in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, Mari Yamaguchi in Tokyo and global executive producer Kiko Rosario in Bangkok contributed to this report.

item.title: 以色列和伊朗自四月停火以来首次交火后似乎暂停了袭击
item.description:
DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Israel and Iran appeared to back away from further strikes Monday, hours after they traded fire for the first time since the U.S. agreed to a ceasefire with Tehran two months ago. Both countries warned that they were ready to launch retaliatory attacks if provoked.
The renewed hostilities raised concerns that the Middle East could plunge back intoa full-scale war.
Since the U.S. and Israel began striking Iran on Feb. 28, the war hasshaken the global economy, driven up energy prices around the world and made many basics,including food, more expensive. Officials have been unable to turnthe April ceasefireinto a deal to permanently end the conflict.
The new attacks prompted U.S. President Donald Trump to call for an immediate stop to fighting between Israel and Iran.
Soon after, the Iranian military’s joint command issued a statement that said it was halting offensive strikes. The statement said further “aggression and hostile acts” by Israel and its supporters, including in southern Lebanon, would be met with “much more severe and crushing measures than before.”
Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, speaking in a videotaped statement, implied that the current round of fighting was over. But he also warned that if Iran “makes the mistake and returns to attacking us, we will respond with force.”
Netanyahu said Israel is continuing to operate in Lebanon against the Iranian-backed militant group Hezbollah, and that Israel “has full right to self-defense, and we will exercise it to the full extent necessary.”
Both countries lifted restrictions they had imposed as safety precautions. The Israeli military said most schools in Israel that closed Monday would reopen. Iran’s official Mizan news agency reported that the Islamic Republic had lifted airspace restrictions affecting civilian flights.
During the truce, Iran has maintained its stranglehold on theStrait of Hormuz— a crucial passage for the world’s oil and natural gas whose closure was the primary reason global fuel prices skyrocketed. Israel has continued to strike Hezbollah, Iran’s ally in Lebanon, andpushed deeper into that country.
The U.S. military continues to impose ablockade on Iranian ports. U.S. Central Command said its forces on Monday fired on and disabled a Palau-flagged oil tanker, the M/T Marivex, in the Gulf of Oman after the ship attempted to breach the blockade.
Officials in India said the tanker’s crew of 24 Indian sailors were all reported safe after a fire broke out on the vessel. It was the seventh commercial vessel the U.S. military has disabled to enforce its blockade, which began in mid-April.
Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif expressed concern Monday over the surge in violence. In a post on X, Sharif urged all parties to “exercise restraint and give peace a little more chance.”
Two regional officials said Egypt, Saudi Arabia, Turkey, Pakistan and Qatar had all urged the Trump administration to pressure Israel to halt strikes onIran and Beirut.
Those countries also pushed for Iran to stop attacking Israel, the officials said. Both spoke on condition of anonymity because they were not authorized to speak to reporters.
Iran’s ambassador to the United Nations said Monday that Iran and the U.S. are still working to negotiate a peace deal. After speaking at a U.N. Security Council meeting, Amir Saeid Iravani told The Associated Press he was hopeful that “very soon” the two sides would reach “a conclusion.”
Iran launched waves of attacks on Israel on Monday, and Israel launched strikes on central and western Iran.
Iranian state media reported at least 15 people were wounded after explosions sounded in Tehran and other cities. There were no immediate reports of fatalities.
The semiofficial Fars and Mehr news agencies said Israeli strikes hit a petrochemical factory in the city of Mahshahr. They did not elaborate on any damage. The Israeli military confirmed the strike on the plant, saying it targeted sites that produce materials for ballistic missiles. Israel said it also targeted truck-based missile launchers.
Israel said its strikes were in response to an Iranian missile attack. Tehran warned Sunday that it would retaliate after Israel struck Beirut’s southern suburbs without warning. When Israel struck back, Iran fired again.
Explosions could be heard in central Israel as air defenses sought to intercept incoming Iranian fire. Iran’s paramilitary Revolutionary Guard said it had targeted two military bases in Israel.
Iran blamed the United States for the escalation.
“No one believes that the Israeli regime would take any action without coordination with the United States,” Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman Esmail Baghaei told journalists in Tehran.
Trump and Netanyahu launched the war in aclosely coordinated attack, with Israeli officials proudly boasting of unprecedented “shoulder to shoulder” cooperation.
The conflict reached 100 days on Monday, and the two leaders have moved in opposite directions, withtensions sometimes spilling out into the open.
Netanyahu appears to have openly defied Trump with the strike Sunday in Beirut and subsequent attacks in Iran. Trump has voiced his displeasure with Israel, including belittling Netanyahu by declaring to the Financial Times that “I call all the shots.”
Their differences appear to be rooted in each leader’s domestic considerations. Netanyahu faces elections this fall and is under public pressure to strike back against ongoing Hezbollah attacks on northern Israel. He also is wary of appearing too subservient to Trump.
The U.S. president, meanwhile, also faces elections — for Congress in November — and is eager to end a war that has jolted the global economy and raised prices for consumers.
Yemen’s Iran-backed Houthi rebels claimed an attack on Israel on Monday and said Israel-affiliated vessels would again be a target in the Red Sea, putting the waterway in danger along with the Gulf of Aden and the narrow Bab el-Mandeb Strait connecting them. The statement from Brig. Gen. Yahya Saree was broadcast on the Houthis’ al-Masirah satellite news channel.
The Houthis made a similar threat during the Israel-Hamas war in the Gaza Strip and launched attacks that killed at least nine mariners and sank four ships. They often targeted vessels with tangential or no ties to Israel.
The assaults upendedshipping in the Red Sea, through which about $1 trillion of goods passed each year before the war.
This story has been updated to correct the day the Iran war started to Feb. 28.
Lidman reported from Tel Aviv, Israel. AP journalists Munir Ahmed in Islamabad; Matthew Lee in Washington; Michelle L. Price in Bridgewater, New Jersey; Elena Becatoros in Athens, Greece; Samy Magdy in Cairo; Josef Federman in Jerusalem; Edith M. Lederer at the United Nations; and Russ Bynum in Savannah, Georgia, contributed.

item.title: 当局称，纽约宾州车站发生刺伤事件，造成六人受伤，一名嫌疑人已被拘留
item.description:
纽约（美联社）——当局称，周日晚间，纽约宾州车站内发生一起刺伤事件，造成六人受伤，不到一天前，预计将有数千名球迷前往邻近的麦迪逊广场花园观看 NBA 总决赛第三场比赛。
袭击发生在晚上 7 点左右，一名嫌疑人被拘留。位于全国最繁忙的交通枢纽之一。麦迪逊广场花园下方庞大的铁路综合体为美国铁路公司、长岛铁路、新泽西州交通和纽约市地铁线路提供服务，每天有数十万通勤者和旅客使用。
据该市消防部门称，医护人员发现一名受重伤的受害者、两名中度受伤的受害者和两名轻伤的受害者。所有五人都被送往贝尔维尤医院。另一人的情况尚不清楚，已被送往另一家医院。
当局没有立即公布导致刺伤事件或受害者是否成为袭击目标的详细信息。
到周日晚些时候，眼前的混乱已经被宾夕法尼亚车站熟悉的场景所取代。旅客推着行李经过 5 号和 6 号轨道附近的一个用绳子围起来的区域，那里的地板上仍然残留着医用手套、废弃的绷带和血迹。
当纽约准备数十年来最大的体育赛事之一时，暴力事件爆发。周一晚上纽约尼克斯队和圣安东尼奥马刺队之间的对决将是自 1999 年以来第一场在麦迪逊广场花园举行的 NBA 总决赛比赛，预计将吸引大量观众前往球场。
唐纳德·特朗普总统表示，他计划参加全国电视转播的比赛，这促使特勤局、纽约警察局和麦迪逊广场花园及周边街区周围其他机构进行了广泛的安全保护。当局没有立即表明刺伤事件是否会影响比赛的安全计划。
美铁警方表示，他们正在调查这起刺伤事件，并已拘留一名嫌疑人。当局没有立即公布嫌疑人的身份或提供有关潜在指控的信息。

item.title: 随着新的偏远螺旋虫病例被发现，食肉牛寄生虫已蔓延到德克萨斯州以外的地区
item.description:
美国农业部周一宣布，又确认了两例新世界螺旋虫病例，其中一例位于德克萨斯州主要感染群之外，这表明阻止这种可能破坏美国养牛业的害虫是困难的。
螺旋虫实际上是一种苍蝇幼虫，它吃活肉而不是死物。雌性将卵产在牛等温血动物的开放性伤口中，但野生动物、宠物，有时甚至人类也可能受到感染。政府计划培育不育雄性苍蝇，并从飞机上投放成群的雄性苍蝇与野生雌性苍蝇交配，几十年来，巴拿马南端的螺旋虫一直受到控制。
到目前为止，已有四例确诊病例：德克萨斯州的三头小牛和来自邻近的新墨西哥州利县的一只狗。美国农业部最初将这只狗报告为德克萨斯州病例，它生活在新墨西哥州，后来被重新分类为该州首例。该动物的旅行历史正在调查中。
上周，在德克萨斯州南部相距几英里的小牛身上发现了前两例螺旋虫病例。第三个是周一在圣安东尼奥西南部的拉萨尔县宣布的。
佛罗里达大学研究苍蝇的昆虫学家爱德华·伯吉斯说，科学家预计未来几天和几周内可能会出现一些新病例，但这并不意味着螺旋虫正在迅速传播。
伯吉斯说：“当发现第一例病例时，每个人都保持警惕，目光更加集中。”“当你寻找某样东西时，你更有可能看到它。”
自 2024 年底在墨西哥发现螺旋虫以来，美国农业部和美国养牛业一直在努力预防螺旋虫感染。美国在 20 世纪 60 年代已经消除了牧场主一年一度的温暖天气祸害。
到目前为止，它的再次出现并没有对牛肉价格产生太大影响，由于美国的奶牛数量减少，牛肉价格已经接近历史最高水平。尽管这种寄生虫会攻击活牛，但它不会感染肉类或水果。还有十几种政府批准的治疗牲畜的药物。
加拿大周五暂时停止从德克萨斯州进口牛、马或其他牲畜。这些寄生虫更喜欢温度至少为 77 华氏度（25 摄氏度）的潮湿地区，这使得它们在北方的夏季更加成为问题。
美国农业部部长布鲁克·罗林斯将于周一下午在德克萨斯州克尔维尔的美国家畜昆虫研究实验室举行简报会后召开有关此次感染的新闻发布会。
伯吉斯说，长期解决方案——培育不育雄性果蝇——还需要几个月的时间。由于野生雌性苍蝇只交配一次，如果遇到的是不育的雄性，随着苍蝇的死亡，疫情最终可能会停止。
美国农业部正在努力提高美国以外工厂的不育蝇产量，并在德克萨斯州建立一个大型蝇工厂。
然而，得克萨斯州农业专员席德·米勒表示，联邦政府的应对措施将花费太长时间，并且有可能削弱养牛业。相反，他说毒饵可以在几个月内消除螺旋虫问题，即使美国农业部和其他专家表示毒饵尚未被证明有效，并且可能会毒害其他苍蝇、动物甚至人类。
“到底什么是好苍蝇？”米勒周一在接受采访时说道。
这个故事已经更新，以反映美国农业部将狗螺旋虫案件修改为新墨西哥州，而不是该机构最初报道的德克萨斯州，并更正了克尔维尔的拼写。
美联社驻爱荷华州得梅因的撰稿人斯科特·麦克费特里奇为本报告做出了贡献。

item.title: 诉讼试图阻止 UFC 在白宫南草坪举行的特朗普生日之战
item.description:
纽约（美联社）——一项联邦诉讼试图阻止即将在白宫南草坪举行的 UFC 综合格斗比赛，这场比赛是为庆祝美国总统唐纳德·特朗普 80 岁生日而举行，也是庆祝美国建国 250 周年的一部分。
公共诚信项目代表两名弗吉尼亚州居民于周六提交的文件称，特朗普政府对 6 月 14 日活动的授权是非法的。该诉讼称，这种批准违反了国家公园管理局禁止在联邦公园用地上举办体育赛事的规定，国会不同意这座高耸的拱门俯瞰活动空间，并且在施工前没有进行环境审查。
原告律师布伦丹·巴卢（Brendan Ballou）表示：“这从根本上说是一种私人、商业、腐败的行为，利用我们最神圣的国家纪念碑谋取私利。”“这就是引发这起诉讼的原因。”
白宫在一份声明中表示，这一法律挑战是“一种蓄意阻挠、毫无根据且拖延”的行为，旨在阻止特朗普主办这场比赛，该活动“与白宫在南草坪举办的其他各种活动以及全年在椭圆形和国家广场举行的适当许可的活动没有什么不同。”
UFC周日没有立即回应置评请求。
工作人员正在南草坪上搭建一个八角形的笼子。特朗普表示，已完成的 UFC 项目将“在白宫前门外建造一个拥有 5,000 个座位的竞技场”。附近椭圆形公园内还将设置额外的大屏幕来播放比赛，UFC 表示计划发放多达 85,000 张免费门票，以容纳两个地点的观众。
八边形及周边建筑是特朗普引领的白宫建筑热潮中的最新项目。

item.title: 特朗普否认伊朗背叛了他“不再发动新战争”的竞选信息
item.description:
新泽西州布里奇沃特（美联社）——唐纳德·特朗普总统驳斥了这样一种观点，即今年对伊朗发动的战争背叛了他在再次竞选白宫时反复强调的“不再发动新战争”的说法。
特朗普周日在美国全国广播公司（NBC）“会见媒体”节目中接受采访时表示，他“不能保证”如果他重新执政，不会发生战争。
“首先，我没有保证不会发生战争。我为什么要建立世界上最强大的军队？”特朗普说道。
特朗普还为一项现已废弃的 18 亿美元基金的计划进行了辩护，该基金本来可以补偿这位共和党总统的盟友，他还重申了他毫无根据的指控，称加州周二初选的旷日持久的计票工作存在大规模欺诈行为。当他对 NBC 的克里斯汀·韦尔克 (Kristen Welker) 的抵制感到沮丧时，他突然结束了采访。
在2024年的竞选活动中，特朗普多次将他的民主党对手描绘成好战分子，并表示他是一位“不会发动新战争”并将带来一个和平时代的总统。
但特朗普周五在威斯康星州录制的全国广播公司采访中表示，作为候选人，“我没有承诺任何事情。”
“我不喜欢这些无休止的战争。这不是一场无休止的战争。我们已经这样做了三个月，”他在谈到2月28日开始的与伊朗的战争时说道。
特朗普表示，他正在“为世界服务”和“为我们的国家服务”，因为他必须阻止伊朗拥有核武器。但在采访的其他地方，特朗普重复了一个矛盾的信息，他说美国去年的袭击“摧毁了”伊朗核设施。
他还为自己在第一任期内退出民主党总统巴拉克·奥巴马与伊朗核协议的决定进行了辩护，他曾严厉批评该协议，但没有就他承诺达成的“更好协议”进行谈判。
特朗普说：“做这些事情需要数年时间。”
加州众所周知的长期计票工作一直吸引着选举阴谋论，自周二选举以来，特朗普在没有证据的情况下声称民主党正在操纵选举。特朗普任命的洛杉矶最高联邦检察官周五表示，他的办公室已启动“多项选举舞弊调查”。
最新统计的倾向民主党的邮寄选票已经蚕食了特朗普首选的州长和洛杉矶市长候选人的得票总数。尽管特朗普经常表示，在计票较晚时投票总数发生变化是欺诈的迹象，但这只是计票过程缓慢的反映。
特朗普在采访中不断声称这是“作弊”和“选举被操纵”的迹象，当韦尔克敦促他提供证据来支持这一点时，特朗普变得越来越沮丧。
“我所要做的就是看看。我所要做的就是看看，”特朗普说。
“但这不是证据，”韦尔克回答道。
“我会听。我会听人们的意见。让我们看看会发生什么，”特朗普回答道。
特朗普为司法部表示现已放弃设立 17.76 亿美元“反武器化基金”的计划进行辩护，该计划是解决特朗普因纳税申报表泄露而对美国国税局提起的诉讼的和解协议的一部分。
代理司法部长托德·布兰奇周三表示，该部门正在取消该计划。这一消息是在该计划被法官暂停后发布的，民主党人和一些共和党人都表示，他们担心该基金缺乏监督以及一月份向参与者支付款项的可能性。2021 年 12 月 6 日，国会大厦发生骚乱。
特朗普告诉美国全国广播公司（NBC），他认为该基金是“一个好主意”，如果不获得批准，他将“感到失望”。
当被问及他是否认为 1 月 6 日袭击警察的人应该得到赔偿时，特朗普说，“我不想这么说，但我必须看到这一点。”然后，他开始对骚乱和冲击国会大厦的人做出毫无根据的虚假指控。特朗普于 2025 年 1 月上任第一天就对 1 月 6 日期间被起诉的 1,500 多人进行了全面赦免。
NBC 的采访是在威斯康星州奇珀瓦福尔斯进行的，随后特朗普准备在与农民举行的圆桌会议上发表讲话。由于一阵阵大雨落在录音所在谷仓的金属屋顶上，采访多次被打断，有时很难听清楚。
最后，韦尔克就和解基金以及他对加州选举的主张向特朗普施压。特朗普提高了声音，开始称韦尔克和媒体“不诚实”，攻击她的可信度，并抱怨他所谓的“虚假、肮脏的媒体”。
当韦尔克试图转换话题时，特朗普继续说下去，两人之间发生了交叉谈话。特朗普结束采访时说：“我们就此结束吧。”他摘下麦克风，对韦尔克说：“谢谢你，亲爱的。祝你玩得开心。”他说他给了采访足够的时间，然后站起来走开了。
韦尔克在广播中表示，她在周六与特朗普进行了交谈，特朗普同意降雨造成了并发症，并表示他将来会再次接受采访。

item.title: 美联社-NORC 民意调查发现，认为民主是国家身份核心的美国人越来越少
item.description:
WASHINGTON (AP) — As the U.S. prepares for an extravagant celebration of its founding principles, fewer Americans see their country as exceptional, a new poll finds.
The survey fromThe Associated Press-NORC Center for Public Affairs Researchhighlights many Americans’ feeling of unease over the future of its representative government — particularly among young people. It presents a jarring contrast as communities around the country commemorate the nation’s 250th anniversary.
Only about one-quarter of Americans say the U.S. stands above all other countries in the world, the new poll found, while 44% say it’s one of the greatest countries in the world, along with some others. About 3 in 10 say there are better countries than the U.S., an increase from 19% inan AP-NORC pollconducted in June 2016.
Americans remain divided about whether diversity is an essential feature of the U.S.'s identity, and agreement about other aspects of the country’s underlying character appears to be eroding, the survey found. Americans are less likely to see a democratically elected government as “extremely” or “very” important to the United States’ identity as a nation than they were just a few years ago. About two-thirds of U.S. adults now say a democratically elected government is highly important to the U.S.’s identity as a nation, down from 80% in 2021.
“It’s not that the democracy part is not working,” said Derricka Wall, 24, of Chickasaw, Alabama. “It’s the people that are actually being put in office that is the problem.”
Wall believes politicians have damaged America’s governing system, which was designed to ensure representation and guard against government misuse.
America, she said, “is not what it used to be. I feel like our founding fathers would be kind of disappointed with how it is now.”
Young adults are much less likely than older Americans to believe the U.S. is special, compared with other nations, the poll found.
About 4 in 10, 44%, of U.S. adults under 30 say there are other countries better than the U.S., compared with 22% of U.S. adults ages 60 and older.
Fewer, too, see democracy as a key element of the U.S.’s identity. Only about half of Americans under 30 believe this, compared with 81% of those 60 and older.
Wall said the people who established the government with co-equal branches thought they were erecting safeguards to keep any one person or group from attaining too much power. But she believes they didn’t foresee how easily those guardrails would crumble if the people in the system stopped enforcing them.
“I feel like they would actually roll out of their graves,” she said. “I feel they would be very disappointed in us.”
The belief that politics isn’t working for everyday people extends beyond the youngest generations. Kent Stage, 62 and a retired senior enlisted man in the Army, is a registered Republican in Indiana. He does not think the current political system addresses the country’s problems. He’d like to see term limits on politicians and more working-class people serving.
“I’ll trust the ambulance-chasing lawyer and a shady used car salesman before I trust the politician,” he said.
Stage, who is also a former Marine, believes public servants make self-serving choices for their families “while mine and yours still got to hit the old grindstone.”
The survey also finds widespread cynicism about America as the land of opportunity. About half of U.S. adults, 51%, say the American Dream — the idea that if you work hard, you’ll get ahead — once held true but does not anymore. About one-third say it “still holds true” while 15% say it never held true.
Jack Hermanson, a 27-year-old software developer in Denver, said his belief in the American Dream changed when he saw his engineer husband struggle to find a job. “That really shattered my impression that if you work hard, you get what you deserve,” Hermanson said.
Only 22% of Americans under 30 say the American Dream still holds true, compared with 46% of Americans ages 60 and older.
Angela Toombs, 31, works at a senior living facility in Atlanta where her clients talk about how easy it was to buy a house while working their first regular jobs in their 20s and are incredulous about the obstacles facing Toombs’ generation. Toombs recently gave up her own apartment to rent a room in order to save money.
Skepticism about the American Dream is more widespread among Democrats and independents, compared with Republicans. Most Republicans, 57%, say the American Dream still holds true, compared with about one-quarter of independents and 17% of Democrats.
Republicans are also much likelier than Democrats to see the U.S. as exceptional. About half of Republicans say the U.S. stands above all other countries in the world, compared with only 7% of Democrats.
Quintin Sharpe, 28, lives in a resort town on Lake Geneva in Wisconsin. A financial planner who is Republican, he said the American Dream remains accessible and he is proud of the country. “It’s been a great experiment.”
“The opportunity is there for those who want to work for it,” he said. Sharpe believes the country is “a meritocracy, and the best ideas, the best work ethic, those with the best succeed regardless of race, skin color, any of those factors.”
He and his wife will celebrate the country’s 250th anniversary watching the fireworks over the lake.
Just over half of U.S. adults — 56% — say a shared American culture and set of values are “extremely” or “very” important to the country’s identity, down from 65% in 2017. Younger Americans are less likely than older ones to say a singular set of values is important to U.S. identity.
But Americans remain sharply divided on the centrality of welcoming diverse perspectives: About half of adults, 51%, say the ability of people to come from other places in the world to escape violence or find economic opportunities is “extremely” or “very” important to American identity, while 55% say this about the mixing of cultures and values from around the world.
Only about 4 in 10 Republicans see the mixing of cultures and values from around the world as central to the country’s identity, compared with 76% of Democrats.
Rose Nunez, 70, of San Antonio, was a small business owner but now is a caregiver for family members. Nunez, who tends to vote for Democrats, said there is an unease and tension that are just beneath the surface, especially focused on Hispanics. She said some people have started carrying their papers showing their immigration status in case they are challenged.
“It is hard to celebrate when the feelings towards immigrants and communities of color are so strong,” she said of the upcoming America 250 celebrations.
She said even citizens are questioned now. If it gets to a point where being naturalized is challenged, “guess what, my mom would be leaving. She’s been living in this country since she was maybe four years old. She’s 93.”
The AP-NORC poll of 2,596 adults was conducted April 16-20 using a sample drawn from NORC’s probability-based AmeriSpeak Panel, which is designed to be representative of the U.S. population. The margin of sampling error for adults overall is plus or minus 2.6 percentage points.

item.title: 国家队表示，克里斯蒂安·埃里克森在丹麦队比赛中再次摔倒后“意识清醒”
item.description:
周日，克里斯蒂安·埃里克森在代表丹麦国家队比赛时再次倒在球场上，目前意识清醒，正在医院接受进一步检查，这一可怕的场景让人想起五年前欧洲锦标赛上他的心脏骤停。
电视画面显示，在丹麦欧登塞自然能源公园举行的丹麦对阵乌克兰的国际友谊赛中，这位34岁的中场球员在第65分钟进行无球跑动时双手捂住胸口。
在下一张电视图像中，埃里克森仰面躺在地上，周围都是一脸忧心忡忡的球员。有人看到乌克兰教练组挥手示意医务人员上场。
几分钟之内，丹麦足协在 X 上发帖称埃里克森“意识清醒，在这种情况下表现良好”。
10分钟后，足协发表了一份更全面的声明，丹麦队医莫滕·博森表示：“克里斯蒂安做得很好，他自己走出了球场。在我看来，起搏器的反应是应有的。”
“他一度失去知觉，但很快就恢复了知觉，我们很快就与他取得了联系。”
博森表示，埃里克森“现在将在医院接受进一步检查，以确定导致事件发生的原因。”
“我们正在与他和医院的医生保持联系，”博森补充道。“但是克里斯蒂安做得很好，他让我向所有球员转达他的问候，并告诉他们他一切都好。”
第 79 分钟，裁判正式宣布放弃比赛——丹麦队 2-1 领先，埃里克森倒地后没有进一步比赛——比赛官员与教练和球员进行了交谈。
当埃里克森接受治疗时，人群中最初一片安静，然后体育场周围响起了“埃里克森，埃里克森”的口号。
比赛取消后，两队球员在半场围成一圈，围绕着两位教练。人们看到教练们正在与球员们交谈。随后，球队在球场上走动，赢得了人群的掌声，但一些球员显然很沮丧。
埃里克森是丹麦有史以来最伟大的球员之一，在 2021 年丹麦在哥本哈根对阵芬兰的欧洲锦标赛小组赛首场比赛中，他面朝前倒地，几个月后，他安装了植入式心律转复除颤器。
在那场震惊足坛的事件中，埃里克森在博森领导的长时间现场医疗治疗中苏醒过来，并表示后来被告知他“离开这个世界五分钟了”。
259天后，埃里克森在英超布伦特福德获得合同后恢复了职业球员生涯。在 2022-25 赛季在曼联效力一段时间后，他加盟德国俱乐部沃尔夫斯堡直至 2026-27 赛季。
沃尔夫斯堡在 X 的帖子中表示，埃里克森已被送往欧登塞大学医院，俱乐部正在关注事态发展。
“祝你一切顺利，早日康复，克里斯蒂安，”沃尔夫斯堡说道。
曼联还发表了一份声明，表示“对丹麦关于克里斯蒂安·埃里克森的最新消息感到鼓舞”。
曼联表示：“在我们等待进一步消息的同时，俱乐部正在向克里斯蒂安和埃里克森家族送去力量和爱。”
丹麦和乌克兰均未获得世界杯参赛资格。

item.title: 国际足联和因凡蒂诺在世界杯前引起了两党的怀疑
item.description:
WASHINGTON (AP) — Dozens of mayors and other city leaders gathered in the ballroom of a Washington hotel on a snowy January morning this year gripped by anger and anxiety about the federal government’s increasingly aggressive immigration enforcement operation that included the killings of two U.S. citizens inMinneapolis.
And then FIFA PresidentGianni Infantinotook the stage.
“For the first time in 250 years of history of the United States of America, well, you will not just be invaded but you will be conquered,” he said as the audience at the National Conference of Mayors largely reacted with silence.
“You will be conquered by soccer,” he added in an attempt to land the joke and get the crowd energized about the World Cup, which will be hosted jointly by the U.S., Mexico and Canada from Thursday through July 19.
Ahead of the tournament, Infantino hassuccessfully cozied upto PresidentDonald Trump, creating a peace prize that was awarded to him and frequently visiting the White House, including a stop last week, when he was photographed alongside the Republican president admiring changes outside the Oval Office.
Infantino has struggled with virtually everyone else.
In a deeply polarized country, few things unite elected leaders outside the White House quite like skepticism of Infantino and FIFA, the governing body for the world’s most popular sport. It’s a sentiment that cuts across the divide and spans from Washington to state capitals and city halls.
There are mayors likeZohran Mamdaniof New York andKaren Bassof Los Angeles, Democrats who’ve balked at ticket prices. Mamdani eventually secured 1,000 tickets for New Yorkers at $50 per seat. The attorneys general in New York and New Jersey, also Democrats, startedan investigationinto ticket prices last month. In New Jersey, Gov. Mikie Sherrill, another Democrat, demanded help fromFIFAto cover millions of dollars in transit costs before ultimately turning to new advertising revenue to help cover the gap.
Despite his ties to Infantino, even Trump has criticized World Cup ticket prices, telling The New York Post he wouldn’t pay the $1,000 prices to watch the U.S. play its opening game against Paraguay.
In an interview, Republican Sen.Todd Youngof Indiana, who played Division 1 soccer at the U.S. Naval Academy, said FIFA has been “detached from regular people around the world.”
“It really is a cabal run by elites,” Young added. “They really have had problems with corruption over the years, and one really does get the sense that they may overlook their singular mission, which is to help grow the sport, especially among young people around the world who wouldn’t otherwise have the resources to access soccer.”
“Every good soccer fan who loves international football wishes Infantino would be a little less in the news and more promoting the game,” Rep. Rick Larsen, D-Washington, said in an interview.
A FIFA representative didn’t respond to a request for comment. FIFA’s skeptics said they were still enthusiastic about the 48-team tournament. Some lawmakers said Infantino was navigating a challenging political environment in the U.S.
Infantino is “doing the job he needs to do in terms of cultivating the Trump administration,” said Rep. Darin LaHood, R-Ill., the chair of the Congressional Soccer Caucus.
The World Cup kicks off a series of events that are central to Trump’s second term effort to burnish his image and legacy through his association with high-profile sporting events. He’ll holda UFC bouton the South Lawn of the White House this month. It all culminates withthe Olympicsin Los Angeles in 2028, the final year of Trump’s presidency.
But the soccer tournament opens against the backdrop of an intensely divided political climate in the U.S. — with Trump at the center. Just 37% of U.S. adults approve of the way Trump is handling the presidency, according to a May poll fromThe Associated Press-NORC Center for Public Affairs Research.
Still, Trump is intensifying efforts to put himself in the middle of American life, particularly as the country celebrates the 250th anniversary of its independence. He has embarked ona massive renovationof Washington and plans to headline “The Great American State Fair” on June 24.
Trump plans to attendthe NBA Finalsbetweenthe New York Knicks and the San Antonio Spurson Monday. As the head of state, Trump is expected at the World Cup final in July.
But the World Cup presents a challenge for an administration that has placed aggressive immigration enforcement at the top of its agenda.
The most visible aspects of Trump’s anti-immigration measures, including high-profile arrests in cities like Chicago and Minneapolis, have largely calmed. And the administration has sought to strike a welcoming stance toward World Cup visitors, suspending, for example,a requirementthat those traveling from countries that qualified for the tournament and have bought tickets pay as much as $15,000 in bonds to enter the U.S.
But the White House is still considering hard-line options to punish perceived opponents. Homeland Security SecretaryMarkwayne Mullinhas threatened to halt customs processing at airports serving cities whose local governments resist Trump’s immigration policies. Andintense clashesat a New Jersey immigration center about 10 miles (16 kilometers) from where the World Cup final will be held are a reminder of the tests facing the White House.
“I see the 2026 World Cup at the intersection of two really stark realities,” said Ashleigh Huffman, who was the chief of sports diplomacy at the State Department during the Biden and first Trump administrations. “Unprecedented opportunity to heal a country that is deeply divided and a world that is struggling. And unprecedented scrutiny. Everything that’s going on has the power to unite us, but it also is forcing conversations around access and human rights and immigration and who gets included in this celebration.”
Speaking to reporters in Miami last week, Andrew Giuliani, the executive director for the White House Task Force on the World Cup, said that “if you’re inside the country legally, then you have nothing to worry about.”
“We want people to be able to come here and enjoy this World Cup while also making sure that we can keep the country safe,” he said.
There are signs that the political divisions that course through so much of American culture also apply to the World Cup.
Democrats and independents are more likely than Republicans to say they plan to watch World Cup games, according to an Ipsos poll conducted in May. Earlier polling found Democrats were more likely to be “very” or “somewhat” interested in the matches.
While Republicans are less eager to tune into games, they feel a stronger sense of national pride than Democrats do from the U.S. team’s performance and participation in the World Cup. About two-thirds of Republicans said the U.S. team’s participation makes them proud to be American, compared with slightly less than half of Democrats.
But for soccer enthusiasts in Washington, the hope is that the tournament could provide a rare break from the constant partisan battle.
“There’s a real opportunity to use this platform as a stage for unity and commonality across nations,” Young said.
For Larsen, “when the whistle blows until the end, I’ll be yelling for red cards and cheering goals.”
Associated Press writer Gisela Salomon in Miami contributed to this report.

item.title: 曾效力过三支 NBA 冠军球队的公牛队长期解说员史黛西·金 (Stacey King) 去世，享年 59 岁
item.description:
芝加哥（美联社）——史黛西·金（Stacey King）去世了。1991-93 年，他随芝加哥公牛队连续三届夺得 NBA 冠军，之后又以赢得艾美奖、深受球迷喜爱的播音员身份重返公牛队。他59岁了。
公牛队宣布金周日去世，并表示他们接到了家人的通知。目前还没有其他细节。
公牛队主席杰里·雷因斯多夫在一份声明中表示：“史黛西·金是公牛队大家庭中一位珍贵的成员，也是我们组织历史上真正独特的人物之一。”“他与芝加哥、公牛队和我们球迷的联系跨越了三十多年——首先是作为一名球员，后来作为明确无误的声音帮助公牛队篮球走进了几代球迷的家中。我们将深深地怀念他，并记住他每天给我们的组织、我们的广播和我们的球迷带来的欢乐、活力、幽默、坦率和热情。我们的思念与他的家人和亲人在一起。”
金在公牛队开始了他的 NBA 职业生涯，公牛队在 1989 年选秀中以第六顺位选中了这位身高 6 英尺 11 英寸的前锋/中锋。在公牛队的五个赛季中，他场均贡献 6.6 分和 3.3 个篮板。
他在职业生涯中总共在 NBA 打了八个赛季，其中还曾效力于明尼苏达州、迈阿密、波士顿和达拉斯。金职业生涯场均贡献6.4分和3.3个篮板。
作为一名广播员，金深受新一代公牛队支持者的喜爱。他担任公牛队比赛解说员长达二十多年。
“史黛西喜欢成为公牛队的一员，”公牛队总裁兼首席执行官迈克尔·雷因斯多夫在一份声明中表示。“你可以在他所做的每一件事中感受到这一点——他打球的方式、他对比赛的方式以及他与我们球迷联系的方式。他有一种独特的天赋，能够将人们聚集在一起，让每场比赛都变得个性化。他在每次转播中都带来了对比赛的能量和热爱，帮助球迷感受到与我们球队的联系。无论是通过转播、对话还是与球迷合影，史黛西都让人们感到被重视和被重视。
“我们很幸运不仅以球员和解说员的身份认识他，而且以朋友的身份认识他。史黛西真诚地关心他人，他让我们的组织变得更好。我们将深深地想念他，他的影响、记忆和遗产将永远成为芝加哥公牛队的一部分。”
芝加哥体育网总裁兼首席执行官迈克尔·麦卡锡称金是“芝加哥体育界最受欢迎的人物之一”。
“史黛西拥有将几代公牛队球迷联系在一起的独特能力，”麦卡锡说。“无论是通过他的篮球洞察力、他的幽默，还是他令人难忘的电话，他让每场比赛都变得更加有趣，每一次转播都变得更好。”
金用他令人难忘的电话和绰号让粉丝们高兴不已。每当公牛队投出一记重球时，他的热情就显而易见。
“我们喜欢我们所做的事情，”金上个月在他的“给我辣酱”播客中说道，这是他在重大比赛后经常发表的评论。“这是一项有趣的工作。这对我来说似乎从来都不是工作。每天晚上，我都去工作，无论赢、输或平，我都很开心。”

item.title: 比尔·马赫吐温奖嘉宾揭晓，肯尼迪中心放弃“特朗普”品牌
item.description:
纽约（美联社）——6 月 28 日在肯尼迪中心举行的颁奖仪式上，比尔·马赫获得马克·吐温美国幽默奖，约翰·梅伦坎普、杰伊·雷诺和惠特尼·卡明斯将担任演讲嘉宾。
该中心周一在发给会员的电子邮件中披露了该名单，会员可享受从 99 美元的座位预订到 1,500 美元甚至更多的座位和演出前和演出后招待会入场券的套餐。该公告还标志着该中心在自我定位方面发生了显着变化：该消息来自肯尼迪中心，而不是“特朗普肯尼迪中心”，这一转变也出现在该中心的主页上。
上个月，一名联邦法官裁定，唐纳德·特朗普总统精心挑选的董事会在投票将特朗普的名字添加到该会场时存在非法行为，国会于 1964 年以最近被暗杀的约翰·F·肯尼迪总统的名字命名该会场。
美国地区法官克里斯托弗·库珀写道：“肯尼迪中心的组织章程明确规定，该中心将以肯尼迪总统的名字命名，并且不能根据董事会的单方面决定使用任何其他正式名称或公共纪念馆。肯尼迪中心的名称是国会赋予的，只有国会才能更改。”
尽管肯尼迪发言人表示该场馆可能会对这一决定提出上诉，但该中心上周收到了总法律顾问的一封电子邮件，其中列出了合作步骤，包括删除特朗普的名字。特朗普在第一个任期内基本上忽视了肯尼迪中心，但很快就将该中心作为其清除联邦文化机构所谓“觉醒”影响的总体使命的关键部分。他罢免了领导层，让董事会充满了支持者，并主持了肯尼迪荣誉仪式等标志性活动。
作为回应，许多表演者取消了在该中心的演出计划，其中包括伊萨·雷 (Issa Rae)、贝拉·弗莱克 (Bela Fleck) 和蕾妮·弗莱明 (Renée Fleming)。
马赫是第 27 位吐温奖获得者，此前该奖项曾授予莱诺、史蒂夫·马丁和蒂娜·菲等人。特朗普和马赫近年来公开不和，但这位 HBO 主持人却是许多共和党人喜欢的那种喜剧演员——一位自封的独立人士，经常攻击好莱坞“觉醒”，并采访过所有人，从简·方达这样的自由派坚定支持者，到本·夏皮罗和查理·柯克这样的保守派评论员，再到伍迪·艾伦这样的“被取消”的文化人物。
预计出席吐温仪式的其他嘉宾包括喜剧演员路易斯·C.K.（自 2017 年性行为不端指控短暂颠覆他的职业生涯以来，他一直定期巡回演出）以及备受争议的 ESPN 名人斯蒂芬·A·史密斯（Stephen A. Smith），他对体育及其他领域的直言不讳的评论导致与 NBA 球星杰伦·布朗和评论员基思·奥尔伯曼等人发生了激烈的交流。

item.title: A select list of winners at the 2026 Tony Awards
item.description:
纽约（美联社）——以下是 2026 年托尼奖的部分获奖者。
改编自 Apple TV 电视剧的《Schmigadoon!》赢得了最佳新音乐剧奖，80 岁的演员约翰·利思戈 (John Lithgow) 成为有史以来赢得托尼奖最佳表演奖的最年长男性。（6月8日）
劳里·梅特卡夫《阿瑟·米勒的推销员之死》
扎隆·莱文斯顿和比尔·劳奇，《猫：杰利克舞会》
乔·曼特洛《阿瑟·米勒的推销员之死》
“施米加杜！”（音乐和歌词：Cinco Paul）
《猫：杰利克球》，奥马里·怀尔斯和阿图罗·里昂
《阿瑟·米勒的推销员之死》乔·曼特洛
有关 2026 年托尼奖的更多报道，请访问 https://apnews.com/hub/tony-awards。

item.title: 《恐怖电影》斩获票房冠军，击败《宇宙大师》并延续低预算势头
item.description:
纽约（美联社）——夏季票房正在蓬勃发展——但这并不是因为通常的嫌疑。
据周日电影公司估计，在独立恐怖电影占据票房冠军三周后，恐怖恶搞电影《恐怖电影》周末票房收入达到 5500 万美元，轻松击败了远非强大的《宇宙大师》。
电影院最近出现了新的秩序，Z世代的购票者纷纷涌向恐怖片《痴迷》和《密室》，这两部影片都是由 YouTuber 转型为电影制片人制作的。这些电影的光芒甚至超过了华特迪士尼公司的《星球大战：曼达洛人和格罗古》。
这个周末，喜剧是失败者的冠军。尽管这一类型的电影在影院中几乎已经消亡，但第六部《恐怖电影》在全球上映的票房收入为 1.055 亿美元，创系列最高纪录。韦恩斯兄弟的喜剧甚至超越了其主要讽刺目标“尖叫”系列。今年早些时候，《惊声尖叫7》全球首映票房为9700万美元。
尽管米拉麦克斯制作了新的“恐怖电影”，但这两个系列均由派拉蒙影业发行。这部续集由马龙、肖恩、基南和克雷格·韦恩斯共同编剧，标志着韦恩斯夫妇在 2001 年的《恐怖电影 2》后因创意分歧而退出该系列。
“对于这部喜剧续集来说，这是一个出色的开场，”电影咨询公司 FranchiseRe 的经营者大卫·A·格罗斯 (David A. Gross) 说道。“继 2013 年最后一集安娜·法瑞丝和雷吉娜·霍尔被排除在外之后，这是一个巨大的反弹。周末的数字是该类型平均水平的三倍。”
评论不好（烂番茄上有 26% 的新鲜度），观众评分（CinemaScore 为“B”）也一般。但这并没有阻止这部耗资 3000 万美元的《恐怖电影》在预算更大的竞争中占据主导地位。
根据 20 世纪 80 年代动画系列和美泰玩具改编的剑与魔法动作冒险片《宇宙大师》未能重振这个休眠系列。亚马逊米高梅发行的《宇宙大师》电影是继 1987 年同名电影之后的第二部，国内首映票房为 2,930 万美元。
由尼古拉斯·加利津 (Nicholas Galitzine) 饰演希曼 (He-Man) 主演的《宇宙大师》(Masters of the Universe) 海外票房收入达 2500 万美元。但对于一部制作成本近 2 亿美元的电影来说，需要更高的发行量才能实现盈利。
这是美泰工作室自 2023 年推出《芭比娃娃》以来的首个作品。但在该电影获得 14.5 亿美元的巨大成功之后，对于这家玩具公司来说，《宇宙大师》将更接近失败。
A24 的《Backrooms》是上周末的热门影片，但在第二个周末急剧下滑，跌幅达 68%，票房为 2,590 万美元。但根据 20 岁的凯恩·帕森 (Kane Parson) 的 YouTube 系列片改编的耗资 1000 万美元的电影《幕后密室》(Backrooms) 仍然创下了票房纪录。目前，它是 A24 有史以来票房最高的电影，全球票房达到 2.12 亿美元，超过了《马蒂至尊》。
焦点影业出品的《Obsession》在第四周末的票房收入为 2560 万美元，几乎并列第三。26 岁的库里·巴克 (Curry Barker) 的恐怖片票房较上周末下降了 7%。不考虑通货膨胀，没有一部恐怖电影的第四周末表现比这更好。
《痴迷》讲述了一个男人希望他的暗恋对象回报他的感情的故事，制作成本不到 100 万美元。目前，该片的国内票房为 1.521 亿美元，全球票房为 2.248 亿美元，创下了《焦点》的纪录。
在上映的第三周末，《曼达洛人和格罗古》以 1000 万美元的票房一路跌至第六位。它甚至被 Fathom Entertainment 的《神奇数字马戏团：最后一幕》击败，该动画系列的最后两集结合在一起。它筹集了 1270 万美元。
狮门影业的迈克尔·杰克逊传记片《迈克尔》以 8.98 亿美元的全球票房成为该工作室有史以来票房最高的电影。在不考虑通货膨胀的情况下，这使得它在该工作室的《暮光之城》和《饥饿游戏》系列中的票房最高的作品中遥遥领先。
2026年，环球影业迎来了首部票房十亿美元的电影。《超级马里奥银河电影》全球票房突破10亿美元。
根据 Comscore 的数据，整个周末的房价比去年同一个周末上涨了 63%。全年门票销量增长超过 13%。下周末，史蒂文·斯皮尔伯格的《披露日》首映。
根据 Comscore 的数据，随着周一公布的最终国内数据，该列表考虑了美国和加拿大影院周五至周日的预计门票销售情况：
2.《宇宙大师》，2930万美元。
5.《神奇数字马戏团：最后一幕》，1270万美元。
6.《星球大战：曼达洛人和格罗古》，1000万美元。
10.《穿普拉达的女王2》，280万美元。

item.title: 人工智能股票收复了上周的部分抛售，而油价则从隔夜高点回落
item.description:
纽约（美联社）——随着周一股市在人工智能热潮中反弹，华尔街正在收复周五以来的部分抛售。与此同时，在以色列和伊朗之间的战斗之后，油价走高，但已从隔夜的高点回落。
标准普尔 500 指数上涨 0.7%，较上周五下跌 2.6%，为 10 月份以来的最大跌幅。截至下午 2 点 09 分，道琼斯工业平均指数上涨 7 点，即不到 0.1%。东部时间，纳斯达克综合指数上涨 1.3%。
一些表现最好的公司是销售计算机芯片、内存和其他产品的公司，这些产品推动了人工智能的繁荣。由于担心人工智能的狂热导致其价格过高，周五它们的股价暴跌。这些担忧拖累韩国 Kospi 指数周一早盘下跌 8.3%，令三星电子和 SK 海力士等科技股遭受重创。
但随着交易通过欧洲向西转移到纽约，价格回升。美光科技 (Micron Technology) 周五下跌 13.3%，创标准普尔 500 指数最大跌幅，随后上涨 10.5%。该公司股价在 2026 年迄今已上涨两倍多，随后又恢复了上涨势头。
标准普尔道琼斯指数表示，Marvell Technology 的股价已增长到足以加入其广泛关注的标准普尔 500 指数，该公司在其首次交易中股价上涨 14.7%。今年迄今为止，Marvell 股价在上周某一天飙升 32.5% 的推动下也上涨了两倍多。这是自 2000 年开始交易以来表现最好的一天，此前 Nvidia 首席执行官黄仁勋在台湾的一次会议上表示 Marvell 可能成为“下一个万亿美元公司”。
这样的评论可能会立即使一家公司的价值增加数十亿美元，这让批评者认为人工智能股票已经太热了。芯片和内存公司确实因为人工智能的繁荣而获得了收入和利润的大幅增长，但它们的股价却以惊人的速度飙升。例如，截至周四，广受关注的半导体股指数今年迄今已飙升近 85%。
现在的问题是，周五的下跌是否是经济低迷的开始，还是只是一个暂停，动摇了过度乐观的情绪。
摩根士丹利策略师迈克尔·威尔逊相对乐观。他在一份报告中写道：“市场很少以自三月份低点以来的速度直线波动。”“我们认为，如果这次牛市要延续到年底，调整是不可避免的，而且最终是健康的”，并将标准普尔 500 指数拉至 8,000 点的基准目标。这将比周五收盘价上涨 8.3%。
亚马逊宣布达成一项价值数十亿美元的交易，康宁将为其全国各地的数据中心生产光纤、电缆和其他产品，康宁股价上涨 6.5%。
这帮助抵消了金宝汤 1% 的下跌，该公司公布的最新季度利润强于分析师预期，但收入下降幅度也更大。当 Marvell Technology 的股票加入标准普尔 500 指数时，该公司的股票也将退出标准普尔 500 指数。
在以色列和伊朗相互发动袭击、威胁将该地区拖入全面战争后，石油市场价格大幅上涨。国际标准布伦特原油价格隔夜短暂突破 98 美元。
但在伊朗军方表示将停止进攻行动后，局势随后有所缓和。布伦特原油价格最近为每桶 94.14 美元，较周五上涨 1.1%。
与伊朗的战争造成的高油价已经推高了通货膨胀，这不仅增加了家庭开支，还增加了债券市场的收益率。全球范围内的高收益率最近威胁到经济放缓，并压低股票和各种其他投资的价格。
继周五大幅上涨后，周一美国国债收益率基本保持稳定。10年期国债收益率稳定在4.55%。
在国外股市，继亚洲股市大幅下跌后，欧洲股指小幅走低。
日本政府将今年第一季度的年化经济增长率修正为 1.8%，低于此前预测的 2.1%，日本日经 225 指数下跌 3.8%。
上海股市和香港股市也分别下跌 1.7% 和 1.2%。
美联社商业作家 Yuri Kageyama 和 Matt Ott 对本报告做出了贡献。

item.title: 苹果在上一次与首席执行官蒂姆·库克（Tim Cook）举行的开发者大会上推出了以隐私为重点的新人工智能功能
item.description:
加利福尼亚州库比蒂诺（美联社）——苹果公司周一在年度开发者大会上推出了新的人工智能功能，重点关注隐私和日常工作，这是首席执行官蒂姆·库克 (Tim Cook) 在 9 月份将职位移交给约翰·特努斯 (John Ternus) 之前的最后一次大会。
虽然这家标志性的 iPhone 制造商在人工智能方面一直在追赶竞争对手，但它试图通过强调以隐私为中心的方法以及在其设备和应用程序中集成人工智能来将自己与同行区分开来。
库克获得了长时间的起立鼓掌，他告诉观众，他“非常感谢与你们一起踏上这段旅程”，并表示“围绕苹果平台的能量从未如此强大。”
周一，全球开发者大会在苹果硅谷总部召开，来自约 65 个国家的数千名开发者参加，会议重点关注软件，这与秋季发布的最新 iPhone 形成鲜明对比。
库克于 4 月宣布退休，结束了在 iPhone 推动的繁荣时代，该公司市值飙升超过 4 万亿美元的 15 年连续走势。Ternus 在过去 25 年里一直在苹果公司工作，其中包括过去五年负责 iPhone、iPad 和 Mac 底层的工程设计，这个职位使他成为接替库克的主要候选人。
对于苹果来说，新任首席执行官的过渡正值关键时刻。自 2007 年乔布斯推出第一部 iPhone 以来，人工智能在行业内引发了最剧烈的变革。苹果在人工智能领域的开局并不顺利，此前苹果公司在提供基于该技术的新功能方面遭遇了挫折，正如近两年前所承诺的那样。
